content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

107 SOUTH BROADWAY • • • SUITE 7007 • • • LOS ANGELES, 90012

March 12, 1979


Mr. Michael Dunn
Elmhurst Presbyterian Church
1332 98th Avenue
Oakland, California 94603

Dear Mr. Dunn:

     This letter is to confirm our telephone conversation of
March 7, 1979 concerning the itemization of contributions
received from churches in excess of $100.

     Government Code Section 84210(g) specifies in part that...
"the full name of each person from whom a contribution or contributions totaling one hundred dollars ($100) or more has been
received, together with his street address, occupation, and the
name of his employer, if any, or the principal place of business
if he is self-employed, the amount he contributed, the date
on which each contribution was received during the period
covered by the campaign statement, and the cumulative amount
he contributed."

     Therefore, if a contribution of $100 or more is received
and drawn on a church's account, the church has to be itemized
as the donor on the recipient committee's campaign report.

     I have enclosed a copy of the Political Reform Act for your
information. If our office can be of any further assistance,
please do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

Helen P. Andino
Special Compliance
Representative

HPA:jg

Enclosure


SACRAMENTO (916) 322-5660 • LOS ANGELES (213) 620-5196

sections.conclusion: Therefore, if a contribution of $100 or more is received and drawn on a church's account, the church has to be itemized as the donor on the recipient committee's campaign report.
sections.question_synthetic: Must a church be itemized as the donor when contributions of $100 or more are received from a church account?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Yes, when contributions of $100 or more are received from a church account, the church must be itemized as the donor on the recipient committee's campaign report in compliance with Government Code Section 84210(g).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION section headers present; document references prior telephone conversation. Conclusion extracted from substantive paragraph. Question synthesized from letter content and referenced statute.

CONCLUSION: Therefore, if a contribution of $100 or more is received and drawn on a church's account, the church has to be itemized as the donor on the recipient committee's campaign report.

embedding.summary: FPPC Special Compliance Representative Helen P. Andino confirms that churches must be itemized as donors when contributions of $100 or more are received from church accounts, pursuant to Government Code Section 84210(g) itemization requirements.
